trickyProperty: Trailing acute é (U+00E9) as the final, most information-dense char of a single token; catalog slugs as 'beyonce'. ASCII-strip truncates to 'Beyonc '; .NET \w keeps é unfolded so it never matches the folded index.
expectedHandling: NFD-decompose then strip NonSpacingMark to fold é→e; emit BOTH 'Beyoncé' and 'beyonce'. Never replace an accented letter with whitespace; compare accent-insensitively.

trickyProperty: Iconic 5-letter single token with ö right after the anchor consonant; no redundancy for fuzzy recovery. ASCII-strip splits 'Bj rk'.
expectedHandling: Fold ö→o in place → 'bjork' as one token; emit {'Björk','bjork'}. Collapse accents in place, never to whitespace, especially for short names.

trickyProperty: Grave è (U+00E8), distinct from acute; a hand-built fold table listing é but forgetting è leaves it unmatched. Proves the fold must cover the whole accent family.
expectedHandling: Prefer an NFD+strip-marks pass over a hand list so grave/acute/circumflex/tilde/macron all fold uniformly → 'mylene farmer'; emit {raw, folded}.

trickyProperty: Leading uppercase É (U+00C9); ASCII-strip yields a leading space + 'dith', breaking any prefix match. Uppercase accents are easy to omit from a lowercase-only fold table.
expectedHandling: Fold É→E, trim leading/trailing whitespace defensively; ensure the fold covers both É and é → 'edith piaf'.

trickyProperty: Mid-word decorative ö (U+00F6); pronounced 'Motorhead', slug 'motorhead'. ASCII-strip splits into 'mot'/'rhead'.
expectedHandling: Fold ö→o via NFD → single contiguous 'motorhead'; emit folded alongside raw. Decorative umlauts join tokens, never break them.

trickyProperty: Two decorative umlauts across two tokens (ö, trailing-ish ü). A folder handling only the first accent leaves the second; ASCII-strip yields 'M tley Cr e'.
expectedHandling: Fold every accented letter (ö→o, ü→u) preserving word boundaries → 'motley crue'; emit {raw, folded} plus artist-only fallback.

trickyProperty: Mid-token ë (U+00EB) in a single-word stage name; any split is fatal. ASCII-strip → 'ti'/'sto'. Same class as the degree-sign bug.
expectedHandling: Fold ë→e → 'tiesto' as one token; emit {raw, folded}. Single-token names must never be whitespace-split by sanitization.

trickyProperty: å (U+00E5, ring) DOES NFD-decompose, deliberately contrasting ø/æ which do not. A map-only folder misses å/ö; an NFD-only folder misses stroke letters. Proves you need both.
expectedHandling: NFD+strip-marks handles å→a, ö→o → 'hakan hellstrom'; pair the NFD pass with an explicit non-decomposable table.

trickyProperty: Czech ř (caron) + á, both decomposable; classical metadata arrives mixed NFC/NFD so [^\w\s] keeps base letter AND standalone combining mark, making byte-different 'dvořák' forms unequal. Breaks classical search at scale.
expectedHandling: NFC-canonicalize for dedup, then NFD+strip-marks → 'antonin dvorak'; emit {raw, folded}.

trickyProperty: Icelandic æ (U+00E6) has NO NFD decomposition; NFD+strip-marks silently leaves it. Catalog folds 'ágætis'→'agaetis' (æ→ae).
expectedHandling: Apply an explicit ligature map (æ→ae) in addition to NFD → 'agaetis byrjun'. Maintain a hardcoded table for the non-decomposing set {æ,œ,ß,ø,ð,þ,ł,đ,ı}; emit {raw, folded}.

trickyProperty: French œ (U+0153) has no NFD decomposition; NFD leaves it, ASCII-strip deletes it ('cur de pirate'). Catalog slug 'coeur-de-pirate'.
expectedHandling: Explicit map œ→oe → 'coeur de pirate'; emit {raw, folded}. Same hardcoded-ligature-table requirement as æ.

trickyProperty: Leading Æ ligature (U+00C6) standing for 'Ae'; ASCII-strip deletes the first sound → ' nima'. NFD does not decompose it.
expectedHandling: Expand-then-strip: Æ→Ae BEFORE sanitization → 'Aenima'; keep raw 'Ænima'. Never strip non-ASCII letters to space.

trickyProperty: ß (U+00DF) folds to TWO letters 'ss', no NFD decomposition; ü decomposes. Groß+stadt yields triple-s 'grossstadt'. ToLowerInvariant leaves ß intact so casing alone never expands it.
expectedHandling: Explicit ß→ss expansion plus NFD for ü → 'grossstadtgefluster'; treat ß as length-expanding (1→2) and do length-sensitive work after expansion.

trickyProperty: ToUpper('ß')→'SS' (1→2, shifts indices); distinct capital ẞ (U+1E9E) exists; NFC/NFD never touch ß. A normalize-only pipeline keeps 'weißes' and misses slug 'weisses-fleisch'.
expectedHandling: Apply ß→ss (ẞ→SS) as part of casefold BEFORE invariant-lowercasing so 'Weißes'/'Weisses'/'WEISSES' all key to 'weisses'; emit both forms.

trickyProperty: Ø (U+00D8) is a stroke letter, NOT 'O+mark', no NFD decomposition; 2-char name has zero redundancy. ASCII-strip → bare 'M'.
expectedHandling: Explicit map ø→o, Ø→O → 'mo'. Stroke letters {ø,đ,ð,ł,ħ} need the hardcoded table; guard degenerate 1-char queries by also emitting raw 'MØ'.

trickyProperty: Polish ł (U+0142) is a stroke letter with NO NFD decomposition (ą/ę/ć/ś/ż DO decompose). The canonical 'looks like an accent but is a stroke letter' trap.
expectedHandling: Explicit map ł→l, Ł→L → 'dawid podsiadlo'; verify per-letter rather than assuming all Polish diacritics behave alike.

trickyProperty: Icelandic thorn Þ (U+00DE) transliterates to digraph 'Th' (1→2 chars), no NFD decomposition; ASCII-strip → 'eyr'.
expectedHandling: Special-letter map Þ→Th, ð→d → 'Theyr'. Length-changing transliteration must run before any min-length/token-count fallback gate.

trickyProperty: Turkish dotless ı (U+0131), no decomposition; folds to i ('sila'). Under a tr-TR culture a stray ToLower('I')→'ı' re-corrupts ASCII letters elsewhere in the pipeline.
expectedHandling: Map ı→i, İ→I → 'sila'; ALWAYS use ToLowerInvariant/ToUpperInvariant so the Turkish-I rule cannot leak into ASCII.

trickyProperty: İ (U+0130).ToLowerInvariant() returns TWO codepoints 'i'+combining-dot (U+0307); naive code never expects lowercasing to LENGTHEN. Apostrophe adds a second split hazard.
expectedHandling: Fold İ→i explicitly (or NFC then strip U+0307) independent of culture → 'istanbul'; strip residual combining dots; pin the sanitize/lowercase order with a test.

trickyProperty: The é arrives precomposed (NFC U+00E9) OR decomposed (NFD e+U+0301); same name → different bytes → nondeterministic match (Apple/macOS deliver NFD). Apostrophe is a coexisting punctuation concern.
expectedHandling: Normalize input to NFC deterministically FIRST, then NFD+strip-marks → 'sinead'; pin a test feeding both byte sequences and asserting identical output.

trickyProperty: In NFD, 'Jóga' carries an orphan combining acute (U+0301, category Mn) that .NET \w keeps as a word char, so 'strip non-word' leaves an un-renderable token that never matches NFC 'Jóga'.
expectedHandling: NFC-normalize, then NFD+strip Mn for the fold variant → emit {'Jóga','Joga'}; apply identically to the artist token.

trickyProperty: Canonical shipped bug: ° glued between 'n' and 'V' becomes a space ('n V'); Qobuz's slug drops it ('record-nv'). Single-letter tokens miss, album query returns 0, and the artist-only fallback was truncated downstream.
expectedHandling: Emit symbol-removed-adjacent 'Record nV' AND spaced 'Record n V', plus a non-truncated artist-only fallback ('Bleu Jeans Bleu'). Never let a mangled album query be the only query.

trickyProperty: French typography (pervasive on Qobuz) puts a NO-BREAK SPACE (U+00A0) between 'n°' and the number; ° becomes a space AND U+00A0 matches \s, so 'n   5' collapses to 'n 5', missing slug 'symphonie-no-5'. Sibling JS sanitizers leave U+00A0 → silent per-plugin divergence.
expectedHandling: Normalize all \p{Zs} separators (U+00A0,U+202F,U+2009,U+3000) to ASCII space first, then apply the degree-sign dual-variant rule: emit 'n 5' and 'n5'/'no5'; keep composer-only fallback.

trickyProperty: $ substitutes 's' inside one word; she later dropped it so both 'Ke$ha' and 'Kesha' index. $ is also a regex/shell metachar. Strip-to-space → 'Ke ha'.
expectedHandling: Apply a leetspeak map ($→s) BEFORE the generic stripper → 'Kesha' (primary); also emit removed-adjacent 'Keha' and raw URL-encoded. Recover same way for A$AP→ASAP.

trickyProperty: $ between 'A' and 'AP' encodes 'ASAP'; whole family (A$AP Mob/Ferg). Strip-to-space → stray 'A' + 'AP'.
expectedHandling: $→S → 'ASAP Rocky' (primary), removed-adjacent 'AAP Rocky', raw URL-encoded, artist-only fallback. Symbol map must fire before whitespace splitting.

trickyProperty: Trailing '$$' encodes 'ss' (Badass); strip-to-space silently truncates the surname to 'Bada', dropping the discriminating 'ss'.
expectedHandling: $→s per glyph → 'Joey Badass' (primary), removed-adjacent 'Joey Bada' (lower priority), raw URL-encoded, artist-only fallback. Never let trailing glued symbols shorten a token.

trickyProperty: ! between 'P' and 'nk' stands for 'i' (Pink); catalog indexes both 'P!nk' and 'Pink'. Strip-to-space → 'P nk' (split); strip-to-nothing → 'Pnk'.
expectedHandling: Emit removed-adjacent 'Pnk', a name-specific '!'→'i' variant 'Pink', raw 'P!nk' URL-encoded, and spaced 'P nk' last; never space-replace a symbol between two word-chars. Artist-only fallback.

trickyProperty: ! between letter 'H' and digit '3' (both \w); slug '3oh3'. Strip-to-space → '3OH'/'3' splits the indivisible name.
expectedHandling: Removed-adjacent '3OH3' (primary, matches slug), raw URL-encoded, spaced fallback. Digits survive as \w; the punctuation between them must be removed-adjacent, not spaced.

trickyProperty: '!' glued to the first official token; slug drops it ('panic-at-the-disco'). Strip-to-space → 'Panic  at the Disco' double-space; exact-string boost lost.
expectedHandling: Delete glued punctuation (no space) → 'Panic at the Disco'; collapse whitespace; also emit a punctuation-preserved variant for catalogs that keep '!'.

trickyProperty: Here '!' genuinely separates words, the OPPOSITE of Ke$ha. No single rule knows a priori whether a glued symbol is intra-word or inter-word. Also carries accent é.
expectedHandling: ALWAYS emit BOTH symbol-removed-adjacent AND symbol-to-space variants and let the catalog decide; also fold é→e ('Tony Toni Tone'); always-present artist-only fallback. Proof the corpus must test both removal modes.

trickyProperty: '/' between 'AC' and 'DC' is also a URL PATH separator; raw interpolation forges a path segment → 404, not a search. Slug 'acdc'; display 'AC/DC'.
expectedHandling: Put the value in the QUERY component and URL-encode '/'→%2F; emit {'AC/DC','AC DC','ACDC'}. Prefer query-param search over path-based for names with '/'.

trickyProperty: Internal '/' in an album title (Elliott Smith); when concatenated 'Elliott Smith Either/Or' the '/' is misparsed as a path boundary.
expectedHandling: Component-encode '/'→%2F within the query value; emit 'Either Or' and 'EitherOr'; keep artist+album as one encoded param and a non-truncated artist-only fallback.

trickyProperty: Dots glue 'will'+'i'+'am' into the pronounced 'william'; the real qobuz regex KEEPS dots so 'will.i.am' still mismatches the de-dotted slug. Strip-to-space → three tiny tokens.
expectedHandling: Emit dot-removed-adjacent 'william' (the match), keep raw 'will.i.am', and dot→space 'will i am' last. Generalize: any dotted run between word-chars gets a de-dotted-concatenated variant.

trickyProperty: Dots glue 'm.A.A.d' into 'maad'; appears in album 'good kid, m.A.A.d city' with a comma too. Strip-to-space → four one-letter tokens.
expectedHandling: Dot-removed-adjacent 'maad city' (the catalog match), raw preserved, dot→space last. Case-fold for comparison, keep literal for exact-match.

trickyProperty: Dotted initialism with a TRAILING glued period; commonly also 'REM'. Strip-to-space → 'R E M' three single-char tokens that short-token filters discard, gutting the query.
expectedHandling: Emit removed-adjacent 'REM' and spaced 'R E M' plus raw; do not drop single-char tokens that reconstruct a known acronym; handle the trailing dot adjacently (no dangling token).

trickyProperty: Straight ' between 'B' and 'Day'; catalog indexes 'B'Day' and 'BDay'. Curly ' (U+2019) is an identical separate hazard. Strip-to-space → 'B Day' matches generic 'day'.
expectedHandling: Normalize curly→straight first, then delete the apostrophe without a space → 'BDay' (primary), raw preserved, spaced last; pair with artist so 'BDay' isn't a bare ambiguous token.

trickyProperty: Apostrophe at a NAME-PREFIX boundary (O'/D'/L'); the delete-vs-space decision flips vs contractions. Qobuz slug 'd-angelo' treats it as a separator. Strip uniformly-delete → 'DAngelo' misses the spaced slug.
expectedHandling: Treat O'/D'/L'/Mc' prefixes as a class: emit BOTH spaced 'D Angelo' (matches slug) AND joined 'DAngelo', plus apostrophe-preserved; canonicalize ',’,ʼ to one form first.

trickyProperty: Possessive 's; Qobuz slugs 'destiny-s-child' (separator), Apple returns one token 'Destiny's Child' where 'Destiny'≠'Destinys'. Same sanitizer over-matches one store and under-matches the other.
expectedHandling: Generate BOTH delete-apostrophe 'Destinys Child' AND separator 'Destiny s Child'; canonicalize quote codepoint; cross-quote equality on compare.

trickyProperty: Trailing elision apostrophe in 'Livin'' (curly U+2019 on Apple); Qobuz slug 'livin-on-a-prayer' deletes it. [^\w\s]→space yields 'Livin ' splitting off a dangling fragment.
expectedHandling: Normalize all apostrophe codepoints to one form, then DELETE an apostrophe flanked by/trailing letters (no space) → 'Livin on a Prayer'; emit curly and straight raw variants; artist-only fallback.

trickyProperty: Saxon possessive 's; MusicBrainz leans straight U+0027, Apple curly U+2019. Strip-to-space emits a junk single-letter 's' token and 'Day'≠'Days'.
expectedHandling: Canonicalize the quote, strip the possessive apostrophe WITHOUT a space → 'A Hard Days Night'; also emit a quote-preserving variant; treat U+2019 and U+0027 as equal.

raw: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
trickyProperty: Possessive 'Pepper's' plus abbreviation period 'Sgt.'. Naive sanitizer spaces both → 'Sgt  Pepper s ...' double space + junk 's'.
expectedHandling: Delete the possessive apostrophe (no space) → 'Peppers'; treat the abbreviation period as removed/internal → 'Sgt'; collapse whitespace; quote-preserved variant; cross-quote match.

trickyProperty: Three apostrophes of two semantics: bracketing elision ''n'' (=and) plus contraction 'Ain't'. Naive sanitizer → 'Rock  n  Roll Ain t ...' riddled with junk 'n'/'t' and double spaces.
expectedHandling: ''n''→'n' (delete apostrophes, keep single spaces); 'Ain't'→'Aint'; collapse whitespace; also emit an 'and'-expanded variant 'Rock and Roll Aint ...'.

trickyProperty: 'o'' elides 'of' as its own token; Prince also appears as 'Sign O the Times'. Strip-to-space leaves a dangling single-letter 'o' and loses the 'of' meaning.
expectedHandling: Delete the elision apostrophe keeping 'o' → 'Sign o the Times'; also generate expansion 'Sign of the Times' and quoted-letter 'Sign O the Times'; normalize curly/straight.

trickyProperty: Leading elision apostrophe (eliding 'A' from 'Around'); Apple U+2019 vs left U+2018 vs straight. A leading ' becomes a LEADING space → empty first token, and EscapeDataString encodes the leading space.
expectedHandling: Strip leading apostrophe and Trim → 'Round Midnight'; treat U+2018/U+2019/U+0027 as one class; also emit expanded 'Around Midnight'.

trickyProperty: "N'" is phonetic 'and'; catalogs split straight/curly and also spell 'Guns and Roses'. [^\w\s]→space → 'Guns N  Roses' isolated 'N' + double space.
expectedHandling: Normalize apostrophe codepoint, collapse 'N''→'N', AND generate spelled-out 'Guns and Roses' / 'Guns n Roses'; cross-quote equality.

trickyProperty: Hawaiian ʻokina (U+02BB MODIFIER LETTER TURNED COMMA), a letter-like apostrophe NOT in the usual smart-quote set; the Common quote map only folds U+2018-201D, so U+02BB survives to [^\w\s]→space → 'Kamakawiwo ole'. Same gap hits U+02BC,U+0060,U+00B4,U+2032.
expectedHandling: Extend the apostrophe class to U+02BB,U+02BC,U+02B9,U+0060,U+00B4,U+2032 (plus U+2018/2019/0027), fold to one form, delete intra-word → 'Kamakawiwoole'; emit spaced + preserved variants.

trickyProperty: Trailing glued period on a 3-letter band name that collapses to the very common word 'fun'; the dot is part of the identity. Bare 'fun' floods the catalog.
expectedHandling: Keep both raw 'fun.' and de-dotted 'fun', but NEVER issue the bare common word as a standalone artist-only query — always conjoin with the album ('fun. Some Nights'). Flag short-token-collapsing-to-dictionary-word as needing mandatory album context.

trickyProperty: Curly “ ” (U+201C/201D) are PART of the official title (Childish Gambino) plus a comma and trailing '!'. [^\w\s]→space → '  Awaken  My Love  ' leading empty token + double spaces, often shipped untrimmed.
expectedHandling: Normalize “ ”→", DELETE surrounding/standalone quotes (no space), collapse whitespace, always Trim → 'Awaken, My Love'; treat quoted and unquoted forms as equal.

trickyProperty: Bowie's title is the word Heroes WRAPPED in quotes; codepoint is the crux (Apple/Qobuz curly U+201C/201D, MB straight U+0022, some bare). Strict compare across forms rejects the only real release.
expectedHandling: Canonicalize all double-quote codepoints (U+201C/201D/0022/2033) to one form, strip leading/trailing quotes, Trim → 'Heroes'; all four forms compare equal; artist-qualify 'David Bowie Heroes'.

trickyProperty: Quotes are INTERIOR (mid-string nickname), not bracketing. Leading “→space makes a leading empty token; closing ”→space makes a double space.
expectedHandling: Normalize the double-quote codepoint, DELETE the quotes (no space) → 'Weird Al Yankovic', collapse whitespace, Trim; cross-quote equality.

trickyProperty: Entire artist name is three '!' (pronounced 'Chk Chk Chk'); zero word-chars → sanitizes to EMPTY. An empty ARTIST has no fallback at all — both legs collapse. Already in the repo's own InlineData.
expectedHandling: Detect empty-after-sanitize and route to an artist-alias map ('!!!'→'Chk Chk Chk') and/or artist-ID lookup; also query the literal '!!!' (catalogs index it). NEVER dispatch an empty query; sanitized output must be validated non-empty.

trickyProperty: Three close-parens ')))' (Sunn-amp logo) glued to 'O', UNBALANCED; parens are in the qobuz KEEP set so they survive and can corrupt slug/regex/URL building. A real word 'Sunn' survives.
expectedHandling: Strip the trailing bracket cluster adjacently → 'Sunn O' (primary) but also keep raw verbatim (catalogs index it); bracket handling must tolerate unbalanced runs (don't throw, don't auto-close); fallback = longest alphanumeric prefix, not empty.

trickyProperty: Sigur Rós's 2002 album is '(' space ')'; parens are in the allow-list so '( )' survives, but the tokenizer strips brackets → empty token. Its tracks are also officially untitled (blank).
expectedHandling: Treat a title with no alphanumeric run as 'no usable signal' → go straight to artist-only search; for untitled tracks search by album + track number; curated aliases ('Untitled') optional. Fold artist ó→o.

trickyProperty: Entire title is U+2605 BLACK STAR (Bowie 'Blackstar'); strip-to-space → empty → SanitizeSearchQuery returns string.Empty. Catalogs index it as both '★' and 'Blackstar'. Exact Bleu-Jeans failure mode.
expectedHandling: Detect symbol-only/whitespace-only post-sanitize and DO NOT emit empty; alias map ★→'Blackstar'; emit {'David Bowie Blackstar','David Bowie ★','David Bowie'}; non-truncated artist-only fallback.

trickyProperty: Justice's debut is the single glyph U+2020 DAGGER ('Cross'); strips to empty. Note '†' (dagger) and '✝' (U+271D Latin cross) are distinct codepoints used interchangeably.
expectedHandling: Alias †→'Cross'; emit {'Justice Cross','Justice †','Justice'}; fold both dagger/cross codepoints; artist-only fallback.

trickyProperty: Crosses (Chino Moreno project) stylized as three daggers; the ARTIST field sanitizes to empty, losing the scoping anchor entirely. Proves the artist field itself must be alias-resolved before query building.
expectedHandling: Artist-alias map †††→'Crosses' (searchable), keep '†††' as a display/literal variant; resolve aliases on the artist field, not just titles.

trickyProperty: alt-J's logo/name is the delta triangle; strips to empty. Two delta codepoints exist (U+2206 increment vs U+0394 Greek delta), visually identical.
expectedHandling: Artist-alias map ∆→'alt-J' (the catalog's textual handle); fold U+2206/U+0394 to one; keep '∆' as a display variant.

trickyProperty: Ed Sheeran 2017 'Divide' (U+00F7); non-\w strips to a space → empty query, the exact shipped failure mode. Catalogs store 'Divide' or '÷ (Divide)'.
expectedHandling: Refuse empty queries; symbol→word lexicon ÷→'Divide'; keep raw '÷' as a literal variant; ALWAYS retain artist-scoped 'Ed Sheeran ÷'/'Ed Sheeran Divide' and a non-truncated artist-only fallback.

trickyProperty: Ed Sheeran 2011 'Plus'; doubly dangerous because raw '+' in an x-www-form-urlencoded query DECODES TO A SPACE at the server, silently emptying the term even without a sanitizer.
expectedHandling: Percent-encode '+'→%2B (never let it ride as bare '+'); map +→'Plus'; emit 'Ed Sheeran Plus' + artist-only fallback. Treat '+' as content, not a separator.

trickyProperty: Ed Sheeran 2021 'Equals'; '=' strips to empty AND is the key/value delimiter so naive concatenation corrupts adjacent params (q==&type=album).
expectedHandling: Lexicon =→'Equals'; URL-encode raw as %3D; emit 'Ed Sheeran Equals'; sanity-check the parameter isn't delimiter-corrupted before sending.

trickyProperty: Ed Sheeran 2023 'Subtract'; '-' strips to empty, and if preserved a leading '-' is a NOT/exclude operator in Lucene/Elastic-style backends, actively suppressing results.
expectedHandling: Map -→'Subtract' as the primary query; never send a bare '-' to an operator-sensitive backend (quote it or drop to artist-only); document punctuation-as-title may be an operator.

trickyProperty: Ed Sheeran 2014 'Multiply' is U+00D7 MULTIPLICATION SIGN, a homoglyph of ASCII 'x' — looks searchable but strips to empty and mishashes against a catalog that stored the real U+00D7.
expectedHandling: Confusable-fold ×→'x' AND map ×→'Multiply'; emit both 'Ed Sheeran x' and 'Ed Sheeran Multiply'; keep raw × literal variant.

trickyProperty: XXXTENTACION 2018 '?'; a non-word char that strips to empty AND, unescaped in a URL, begins the query component / is a regex any-char metachar — corrupts the request, not just returns 0.
expectedHandling: Alias ?→'Question Mark' plus artist+year fallback; ALWAYS Uri.EscapeDataString (→%3F) before it touches a URL; treat symbol-only titles as 'needs alias/fallback', not 'sanitize and send'.

trickyProperty: will.i.am 2013; in a URL '#' starts the fragment (client-side only, NEVER transmitted) so unencoded '#willpower' makes the server receive an EMPTY query. Strip-to-space → 'willpower'.
expectedHandling: Component-encode '#'→%23; emit hash-stripped 'willpower' fallback (catalogs index both); guard the URL builder so '#' can never truncate the query into a fragment.

trickyProperty: Mariah Carey 1998 '#1's': leading '#' fragment delimiter + digit + apostrophe. '#' empties the server query; apostrophe fragments '1's'→'1 s'.
expectedHandling: Component-encode '#'→%23 and apostrophe→%27; emit 'Number Ones'/'No 1s'/'1's' variants; protect against '#' becoming a fragment.

trickyProperty: George Clanton 2015; '%' is the percent-ENCODING escape char itself and '% E'→'%E...' looks like the start of an escape sequence to any decoder, causing mojibake/ordering bugs.
expectedHandling: Encode '%'→%25 FIRST/atomically via Uri.EscapeDataString (never hand-rolled ordering); emit '100 Percent Electronica' and '100 Electronica'; any later decode is a single non-recursive op.

trickyProperty: Restates the '/' hazard at the PATH layer: even percent-encoded %2F, many servers/proxies reject or double-decode encoded slashes (AllowDecodedSlash), so correct encoding can still 404.
expectedHandling: Put names with '/' in the QUERY component, never a path segment; emit 'AC DC'/'ACDC' so search succeeds even where %2F is rejected; prefer query-param search APIs over slug paths.

trickyProperty: GY!BE 1997 debut mixes MUSIC SHARP ♯ (U+266F) and INFINITY ∞ (U+221E) with letters; commonly typed 'F#A#∞' where ASCII '#' triggers fragment truncation. Strip-to-space → 'F  A' loses the meaning-bearing symbols.
expectedHandling: Lexicon ♯→'#' and 'sharp', ∞→'infinity'; fold ♯(U+266F) against ASCII '#'; emit 'F# A# Infinity', 'F sharp A sharp Infinity', raw; UTF-8 percent-encode; band-name '!' alias too.

trickyProperty: John Cage; PRIME (U+2032) and DOUBLE PRIME (U+2033), NOT ASCII '/"', around digits. Strip-to-space → '4 33' loses the minute/second structure; catalogs storing typographic primes won't match ASCII assumptions.
expectedHandling: Confusable-fold ′→', ″→" → both "4'33\"" and '4 33'; add spelled alias 'Four minutes thirty-three seconds'; keep digits adjacent.

trickyProperty: Mariah Carey 2008 mixes '=' with superscript ² (U+00B2, 'squared'); strip-to-space → 'E MC ' losing both; slug 'e-mc2'.
expectedHandling: Normalize superscript/subscript digits to ASCII (²→2, ³→3), drop/keep operator; emit 'E=MC2','EMC2','E MC2' so one matches the de-symbolized slug.

trickyProperty: deadmau5 2010 arithmetic title; some catalogs render the multiply as '×' (U+00D7) giving '4×4=12', so the same album tokenizes two ways. '=' strips to '4x4 12' losing identity.
expectedHandling: Normalize ×→'x', keep digits and literal '='; emit {'4x4=12','4x4 12','4×4=12'}; preserve digit-adjacency so '4x4' isn't split into '4 4'.

trickyProperty: Prince's 1992 'Love Symbol' has no stable codepoint; arrives as 'O(+>', a PUA char, or 'Love Symbol'. Strip-to-space collapses 'O(+>'→'O' (one ambiguous letter).
expectedHandling: Normalize all known renderings to alias 'Love Symbol Album'; emit {'Prince Love Symbol','Prince Symbol Album','Prince'}; guard 'sanitized length < 2' → fall back to alias/artist-only, never a single char.

trickyProperty: M.I.A. 2010 'MAYA' is slashes around one 'Y'. SanitizeSearchQuery converts '/' and '\' to spaces (path-traversal defense) → lone 'Y'; backslashes also break JSON/regex interpolation.
expectedHandling: Alias the slash-art form → 'MAYA'/'Maya'; emit {'M.I.A. MAYA','M.I.A. Maya','M.I.A.'}; same 'post-strip length < 2 ⇒ alias/artist-only' guard. Don't rely on the security slash-strip to produce a usable query.

trickyProperty: Aphex Twin Windowlicker track; a full math formula (Greek Δ/Σ, ∈, U+2212 MINUS not ASCII hyphen). Strip-to-space yields garbage smear; title-similarity is hopeless.
expectedHandling: Recognize formula titles (high non-ASCII ratio, sub/superscripts) and STOP matching by title; fall back to artist + EP ('Aphex Twin Windowlicker') + track position/duration; normalize U+2212→'-' generally. The 'give up on title, use surrounding metadata' tier.

trickyProperty: Bare '&' joins two surnames (='and'); MB often spells 'Simon and Garfunkel'. Strip-to-space → 'Simon   Garfunkel' double space, '&' destroyed not translated.
expectedHandling: Emit literal-'&', 'and', and removed-connective variants; collapse whitespace; canonicalize '&'→'and' on BOTH query and catalog title before similarity; artist-only fallback.

trickyProperty: Combines a list comma AND an '&'; both strip to spaces → 'Earth  Wind  Fire', and a feat-aware splitter may mistake '& Fire' for a featured-artist tail and DROP it.
expectedHandling: Strip/space the comma, emit '&'→'and' variant ('Earth Wind and Fire'); never treat a name-internal '&'/',' as a feat delimiter; canonicalize for scoring.

trickyProperty: Streaming shows '&' but MusicBrainz/Lidarr canonical is spelled-out 'Belle and Sebastian' — the connective itself differs across the two sides of the match, so token-equality rejects the release.
expectedHandling: Normalize '&'↔'and'↔'+' to one sentinel on both strings before comparison; generate the alternate-connective query variant.

trickyProperty: Janis Joplin: 'Me and Bobby McGee' on some releases, 'Me & Bobby McGee' on others — the connective flips at TRACK level where similarity thresholds are tighter, and a dropped '&' costs a whole token.
expectedHandling: Canonicalize '&'→'and' (and '+'→'and') on both query and every candidate track title before scoring; emit both renderings when building the search.

trickyProperty: Ampersand arrives HTML/XML-entity-encoded from API/RSS/JSON-in-XML; a strip-before-decode sanitizer sees a-m-p bracketed by '&'/';' and leaves the bogus token 'amp' ('Simon amp Garfunkel'). '&'/'&' degrade worse.
expectedHandling: HTML-decode FIRST (WebUtility.HtmlDecode) so the entity becomes '&', THEN connective-normalize; never run character-class stripping before decoding; assert no query ever contains the literal token 'amp'.

trickyProperty: Wanted as 'Hall & Oates' but canonical credit is the longer 'Daryl Hall & John Oates' — '&' equivalence compounds with a short-vs-full-name mismatch, so even a perfect connective fix needs a fallback.
expectedHandling: Normalize '&'↔'and', collapse whitespace, AND keep token-subset/artist-only fallbacks so 'Hall & Oates' can reach the full catalog entry.

trickyProperty: '+' = 'and' (also spelled 'Florence and the Machine'); '+' is ALSO the form-urlencoded space, so an unescaped '+' silently becomes a space over the wire. Strip-to-space loses the connective.
expectedHandling: Map '+'→'and'; emit {'Florence + the Machine','Florence and the Machine','Florence the Machine'}; always Uri.EscapeDataString '+'→%2B so the wire value equals intent.

trickyProperty: Blink-182 side project; the '+' is the FIRST char and part of the identity, not a separator. Strip-then-Trim deletes it → bare '44' (wrong term); raw '+44' form-decodes to ' 44'.
expectedHandling: Do NOT strip leading/standalone symbols; component-encode to %2B44; emit 'plus 44'/'Plus 44' and a '44' fallback; never trim a leading '+' as if it were whitespace.

trickyProperty: '+' embedded between letters ('C+C') plus spaces; '+' form-decodes to a space merging 'C C', drifting from slug 'c-c-music-factory'.
expectedHandling: Component-encode '+'→%2B and spaces→%20; emit both 'C+C Music Factory' (encoded) and form-decoded 'C C Music Factory' so either index form matches.

trickyProperty: Capital 'N' wrapped in hyphens (no spaces) is the 'and' connective; slug 'salt-n-pepa'. Hyphen→space → 'Salt N Pepa' loses the slug form and never yields an 'and' variant.
expectedHandling: Emit BOTH a spaced form and a hyphen/no-space adjacent form (slug-style survives) plus an 'and' variant; recognize '-n-' as a connective.

trickyProperty: Comma is part of the stylized appositive name, not a list delimiter; Apple stores 'Tyler, The Creator'. Char-by-char replace leaves 'Tyler  the Creator' double space; a stopword filter may drop 'the'.
expectedHandling: Emit {'Tyler, the Creator','Tyler the Creator'}; collapse whitespace; compare case-insensitively; do NOT strip 'the' as a stopword inside artist names.

trickyProperty: Parentheses wrap a single char inside a 4-char mononym (K-pop group); naive replace → 'f x' two ultra-short tokens that backends drop as too short, emptying the query.
expectedHandling: Prefer the de-bracketed join; emit {'f(x)','fx','f x'} with 'fx' primary; do not discard short tokens for this whole-name case.

trickyProperty: Colon glues two digit groups (JAY-Z); indexed literally '4:44'. ':'→space → '4 44' (lone '4' noise) and the digit-joined '444' some search paths normalize to is never generated.
expectedHandling: Treat a colon between digits as glue; emit {'4:44','4 44','444'}.

trickyProperty: Colon separates series/main from subtitle; all-caps; catalogs split inconsistently (some store just 'POST HUMAN'). ':'→space is fine for the full form but NO pre-colon variant is generated.
expectedHandling: Case-fold; emit full, colon-as-space, and pre-colon 'POST HUMAN' variants; treat ':' as a soft subtitle boundary for fallback, not a hard token kill.

trickyProperty: Colon subtitle PLUS a trailing '(The Chase)' part-label that is part of the official EP title (not edition noise); a paren-as-edition heuristic deletes it, a colon-truncate deletes 'Suite I (The Chase)'.
expectedHandling: Keep the parenthetical (no edition keyword); emit full, colon-as-space, and pre-colon 'Metropolis' (lowest priority); fold the accented artist name separately.

raw: Sufjan Stevens Invites You To: Come On Feel the Illinoise!
trickyProperty: Catalog/cover title is long with a colon and '!', but Lidarr/MusicBrainz canonicalizes the album as just 'Illinois' — wanted query and catalog row barely overlap textually.
expectedHandling: Generate pre-/post-colon halves; recognize catalog vs MB titles can be effectively unrelated, so ALWAYS include an artist-only fallback and rank on artist+year when title similarity is hopeless.

trickyProperty: Sault 2019; '7' is \w so it survives, but a title-ONLY query of '7' matches tens of thousands of releases — failure is over-broadness, not emptiness.
expectedHandling: For 1-2 char / pure-digit titles NEVER issue a title-only query and never fall back to query-only; require artist scoping 'Sault 7' and lean on artist+year+track-count.

trickyProperty: Adele 2011 (also 19/25/30); a min-token-length or year/track-number de-noiser drops '21', collapsing the query to 'Adele', or an unanchored '21' floods the catalog.
expectedHandling: Preserve standalone numeric titles verbatim; never classify a digits-only title as year/track-number; generate quoted exact-title + artist-scoped variants so the number is anchored to the artist.

trickyProperty: Taylor Swift; a four-digit title indistinguishable from a release year. De-noisers that strip 1900-2099 year-like tokens delete the actual title (worsened by the 2014 vs 2023 '(Taylor's Version)' reissues).
expectedHandling: Only strip 4-digit years in edition/parenthetical context, never when the year-shaped token IS the title; keep '1989'; account for '(Taylor's Version)' as an appended edition, not a title change.

trickyProperty: Forced-lowercase, hyphen joins word+number; slug 'blink-182'. Strip-non-word → 'blink 182' splits the canonical token; a casing normalizer corrupts to 'Blink-182'; some pipelines strip the numeric suffix.
expectedHandling: Emit verbatim 'blink-182', de-hyphenated 'blink 182', and removed-adjacent 'blink182'; never strip a numeric suffix from an artist name; compare case-insensitively.

trickyProperty: Single letter fused to a number, no separator (galaxy Messier 83). Camel/alnum splitting → 'M 83', then a min-length filter drops 'M' leaving '83'.
expectedHandling: Do NOT split at letter/digit boundaries; keep fused alphanumeric tokens (M83, U2, B52s) intact; never length-filter the surviving letter.

trickyProperty: Trailing '5' substitutes 'se' ('mouse'); the digit is phonetic. A digit-boundary splitter → 'deadmau 5'; a numeric-noise dropper → 'deadmau'; slug 'deadmau5'.
expectedHandling: Preserve trailing/embedded digits verbatim — no boundary splitting, no numeric stripping; treat 'deadmau5' as an opaque token; exact + artist-scoped matching.

trickyProperty: Number spelled as a word; band also stylized 'Matchbox 20', so catalogs store either form. Naive sanitizers do no word↔digit normalization → 0 results despite an exact conceptual match.
expectedHandling: Generate spelled-word and arabic-digit variants for small numbers ('Twenty'↔'20'); try both before falling back.

trickyProperty: Entire artist name is digits (also 112, 702); a digits-only-token de-noiser removes the WHOLE name → empty artist string → empty fallback → 0 results, the shipped truncation failure mode.
expectedHandling: Never strip an all-numeric token that constitutes the entire name; guard empty post-sanitization (use raw if sanitize empties); query '311' verbatim, artist-scoped.

trickyProperty: Roman 'IV' as the de-facto title (officially untitled); catalogs index 'IV' or '4'. No roman↔arabic normalization → 'Led Zeppelin IV' misses 'Led Zeppelin 4'; lowercasing 'iv' collides with the English word.
expectedHandling: Detect roman numerals; emit an arabic variant (IV→4) and vice-versa; preserve original casing for the roman form; try both. Single-letter roman X/I/V must never be length-filtered (cf. Chicago X→10).

trickyProperty: BTS; Hangul arrives precomposed (NFC 방=U+BC29) or decomposed conjoining jamo (NFD ᄇ+ᅡ+ᆼ). \w keeps both but no String.Normalize means NFD≠NFC → 0 results; no Latin 'BTS' variant generated.
expectedHandling: Normalize to NFC before tokenizing/comparing (Apple returns NFD Korean); generate cross-script aliases {방탄소년단, 'BTS'}; query native first; never reduce to ASCII-only.

trickyProperty: BTS 'HYYH' renders as Hangul 화양연화, Hanja 花樣年華, or English title — zero character overlap across writing systems if Lidarr and the catalog chose different forms.
expectedHandling: NFC-normalize then generate a variant set spanning Hangul + Hanja + English (from Lidarr release aliases); always keep a native artist-only fallback; if sanitize empties the query, fall back to the NFC raw, not emptiness.

trickyProperty: Girls' Generation: Hangul 소녀시대 + romanized 'Girls' Generation' (apostrophe straight U+0027 or curly U+2019) + 'SNSD'. Curly ' is replaced by a space → 'Girls  Generation', differing from slug and straight-apostrophe forms. BOTH cross-script gap AND apostrophe-variance break matching.
expectedHandling: NFC-normalize; emit {소녀시대,'Girls' Generation','Girls Generation','SNSD'}; normalize apostrophe variants (straight↔curly↔removed); emit both apostrophe-removed-adjacent and apostrophe-as-space forms.

trickyProperty: Girls' Generation single '훗 (Hoot)'; native title is ONE Hangul syllable. ASCII sanitizer reduces it to '' keeping only parenthetical 'Hoot' (or empty if parens dropped); a one-syllable query is maximally fragile.
expectedHandling: NFC-normalize and KEEP the native syllable; extract the parenthetical English gloss as a sibling variant ('Hoot'); never let sanitizing yield empty — fall back to raw normalized title.

trickyProperty: Reverse direction: canonical name is lowercase Latin 'aespa', alias is Hangul 에스파; an ASCII-clean sanitizer assumes done, but if Lidarr supplies 에스파 vs catalog 'aespa' there's no bridge. Casing to 'Aespa' can miss a case-sensitive lowercase slug.
expectedHandling: Carry both scripts as aliases {aespa, 에스파} regardless of which Lidarr supplies; compare case-insensitively while preserving the lowercase stylization variant; don't assume ASCII-clean needs no CJK alias.

trickyProperty: BTS's official JAPANESE name (Kanji), distinct from Korean 방탄소년단 and Latin 'BTS'; JP-market rows file under the Kanji. Naive code treats the three scripts as unrelated strings with no cross-walk.
expectedHandling: Maintain artist-alias variants across all scripts an act uses (Hangul + Kanji + Latin) sourced from Lidarr/MusicBrainz aliases; fan out the search. First-class case for K/J-pop libraries.

raw: きゃりーぱみゅぱみゅ
trickyProperty: Kyary Pamyu Pamyu, all hiragana with small kana (ゃ,ゅ) and prolonged-sound mark ー (chōonpu U+30FC, category Lm). A sanitizer that folds dash-lookalikes corrupts ー; no romaji variant is emitted.
expectedHandling: NFC-normalize, preserve ー as a letter (never fold to '-' or strip); emit a romaji alias 'Kyary Pamyu Pamyu' alongside the native form.

trickyProperty: Utada Hikaru mixes Kanji 宇多田 + Katakana ヒカル in one spaceless token; catalogued 'Utada Hikaru', 'Hikaru Utada', or 'Utada'. Western↔Eastern name reversal is invisible to a char-stripper.
expectedHandling: NFC-normalize; emit native + romaji in BOTH name orders + surname-only 'Utada'; rely on Lidarr/MusicBrainz aliases for the romanization.

trickyProperty: YOASOBI 'Yoru ni Kakeru'; spaceless Kanji+hiragana. ASCII sanitizer empties it → degrades to artist-only, losing track specificity; even \w emits one spaceless token with no romaji/English variant.
expectedHandling: Preserve native chars (Unicode-aware class, never ASCII-only); NFC-normalize; generate romaji + official-English ('Yoru ni Kakeru','Into the Night'); if sanitize empties, fall back to raw, not artist-only.

trickyProperty: Tatsuro Yamashita 'Christmas Eve'; the katakana middle dot ・ (U+30FB) is NOT in the keep set so it's replaced by a space, mangling matching against a slug that drops it adjacently ('クリスマスイブ'). Exact degree-sign failure class for CJK punctuation.
expectedHandling: For ・ (and half-width ･ U+FF65) emit BOTH the space-separated AND removed-adjacent forms plus the English 'Christmas Eve' alias — the identical dual-variant rule from the degree-sign fix.

trickyProperty: ZONE; subtitle wrapped in wave dashes 〜 (U+301C) which, via Shift-JIS round-trips, is the SAME visual char as fullwidth tilde ～ (U+FF5E) — raw strings never byte-equal. Mixed Latin+Kanji+Hiragana. 〜 strips to spaces.
expectedHandling: Canonicalize the wave-dash family (U+301C,U+FF5E,U+3030,U+007E) to one representative BEFORE comparison; emit subtitle-stripped, subtitle-with-space, and subtitle-adjacent variants; NFC-normalize the remainder.

trickyProperty: Kenshi Yonezu 'Peace Sign'; the prolonged-sound mark ー (U+30FC) sits mid-word as a vowel-lengthener (category Lm, \w keeps it). A common 'fold ‐‑–—―ー to -' hardening step corrupts it to 'ピ-スサイン'/'ピスサイン'.
expectedHandling: Explicitly EXCLUDE ー (U+30FC) and iteration marks 々/ヽヾ from any dash/punctuation folding — they are letters; NFC-normalize and keep verbatim; emit only a romaji 'Peace Sign' bridge.

trickyProperty: BABYMETAL 'Gimme Chocolate!!' uses FULLWIDTH ！！ (U+FF01); fullwidth LETTERS/DIGITS (ＡＢＣ/１２３) are matched by \w and survive un-normalized so they never equal ASCII forms, and fullwidth （） are stripped while ASCII () are kept — inconsistent.
expectedHandling: Apply NFKC (or targeted fullwidth→halfwidth) so ！→!, ＡＢＣ→ABC, （）→(), ｶﾀｶﾅ→カタカナ before sanitizing; then apply the mid-token dual-variant rule to the now-ASCII '!!'. NFKC is width/compat only — not Trad↔Simp.

trickyProperty: Morning Musume's official name ends with U+3002 IDEOGRAPHIC FULL STOP '。' that is PART of the name; an ASCII or 'strip Unicode punctuation' filter drops it → 'モーニング娘', a different canonical name JP catalogs may not match.
expectedHandling: Preserve non-ASCII letters; emit both '{name}。' and '{name}' variants plus romaji 'Morning Musume'; whitelist trailing ideographic full stops in names — don't equate 'strip punctuation' with stripping name-integral CJK punctuation.

trickyProperty: Vaporwave 'Macross 82-99' in FULLWIDTH Latin (Ｍ U+FF2D), fullwidth digits (８ U+FF18), fullwidth hyphen (－ U+FF0D), katakana prefix. \w keeps fullwidth letters/digits so 'ＭＡＣＲＯＳＳ' never equals ASCII 'MACROSS' — zero matches despite identical-looking text.
expectedHandling: Apply NFKC so Ｍ→M, ８→8, －→-, etc. → 'マクロスMACROSS82-99' before building the search; NFKC is the single most important step for CJK-region metadata and must precede tokenization.

trickyProperty: Jay Chou; Traditional 周杰倫 vs Simplified 周杰伦 (倫 U+502B vs 伦 U+4F26 are entirely different codepoints). NFC/NFKC do NOT bridge Trad↔Simp (semantic mapping, not normalization), so normalize-and-strip never helps.
expectedHandling: Add a Trad↔Simp variant step (OpenCC-style table) so {周杰倫,周杰伦} plus romaji 'Jay Chou'/'Zhou Jielun' are all queried; Han folding is a distinct axis from NFC/width normalization and must run on every text field (artist AND album).

trickyProperty: All-Cyrillic with a space, no ASCII. An ASCII \W→space sanitizer replaces the whole string with spaces → empty query with nothing to fall back to.
expectedHandling: Never down-map Unicode letters (\p{L}) to spaces; NFC-normalize; send native 'Молчат Дома' AND a romanized 'Molchat Doma'; if sanitization empties the string, fall back to the URL-encoded original.

trickyProperty: Visually identical to Latin 'DDT' but Cyrillic De/De/Te (U+0414/0414/0422); ASCII strip empties it, OR ordinal equality against a Latin 'DDT' slug silently rejects a true match.
expectedHandling: Run a Unicode confusables/skeleton fold (or Cyrillic↔Latin homoglyph map) to also query Latin 'DDT'; compare confusable-insensitively, not ordinal; keep the native form primary.

trickyProperty: Cyrillic track by Latin-named, dot-laden artist 't.A.T.u.': the artist's dots explode to 't A T u' while the Cyrillic track strips to empty, so the combined query degrades to a mangled Latin fragment. Two failure modes compound.
expectedHandling: Handle each field by its own script: native Cyrillic track + romanization 'Nas ne dogonyat'; don't shatter 't.A.T.u.' on dots (also try 'tatu'); per-field queries so a stripped field can't empty the whole search; Cyrillic artist-only fallback never truncated.

trickyProperty: Greek with tonos-accented precomposed vowels (Ά U+0386, ί U+03AF). ASCII strip empties it; NFD-then-strip-marks removes the tonos producing a corrupted token unequal to the precomposed-NFC catalog title.
expectedHandling: Keep Greek; normalize to NFC (not NFD-then-strip); add an accent-folded variant ('Αννα Βισση') and a romanized 'Anna Vissi' as ADDITIONAL queries; treat tonos as part of the word.

trickyProperty: If uppercased (ΑΡΒΑΝΙΤΑΚΗ) the trailing Σ casefolds ambiguously to medial σ vs final ς; a culture-insensitive ToLower maps every Σ→σ, wrong for word-final position, so the lowercased query ≠ catalog title.
expectedHandling: Lowercase with Greek-aware casing (final-sigma rule) or compare with Unicode caseless matching treating σ/ς as equivalent; preserve native Greek primary + romanization 'Eleftheria Arvanitaki'; NFC.

trickyProperty: RTL Arabic (Fairuz), caseless/cursive, multiple valid alef/yeh spellings. ASCII \W→space erases it; when joined with a Latin album for a combined query, survivors reorder under bidi into a scrambled string.
expectedHandling: Preserve Arabic; build a folded variant (alef أإآ→ا, yeh ي/ى, ة/ه, strip harakat U+064B-0652 and tatweel U+0640) AND keep the unfolded original; add romanizations ('Fairuz','Fayrouz'); query per-field to avoid bidi reordering.

trickyProperty: RTL Hebrew (Omer Adam) with a word-final letter form (final mem ם U+05DD) distinct from medial mem. ASCII strip empties it; naive final-form 'normalization' or strip-as-non-word breaks matching; bidi reorders a Latin-joined query.
expectedHandling: Preserve Hebrew (final forms are distinct meaningful codepoints — do not fold ם→מ); add romanization 'Omer Adam'; NFC; logical order + per-field queries; treat any niqqud (U+0591-05C7) as part of the token. Map Hebrew geresh/gershayim (U+05F3/05F4) as apostrophe/quote equivalents.

trickyProperty: Thai (Bodyslam) has NO inter-word spaces and stacked combining marks — sara-ii (ี U+0E35) + mai-tho (้ U+0E49). ASCII strip empties it; whitespace-splitting yields one giant token; NFD/char-class stripping removes vowel/tone marks changing the word.
expectedHandling: Don't rely on spaces for Thai boundaries — query the whole run; NFC-normalize and never strip Thai marks (U+0E31-0E4E); add romanization 'Bodyslam'; truncate only on grapheme-cluster boundaries.

trickyProperty: Devanagari (Arijit Singh) with dependent vowel matras (ि,ी) that visually reorder + anusvara (ं U+0902). Stripping combining marks deletes matras/anusvara; trimming by code unit separates a base consonant from its dependent vowel.
expectedHandling: Keep all matras/anusvara/virama; NFC-normalize; query native + the dominant Latin 'Arijit Singh' (Indian metadata is often Latin); operate on grapheme clusters; don't reorder logical-order codepoints.

trickyProperty: Devanagari title with a nukta composition: ग़ = ग (U+0917) + nukta (U+093C), which also exists precomposed — NFC vs NFD spellings differ. NFD-then-strip-nukta turns ग़म→गम (different word).
expectedHandling: Normalize consistently to NFC on both query and comparison so nukta forms unify; never strip U+093C nukta or matras; generate a romanized 'Kabhi Khushi Kabhie Gham'.

trickyProperty: The 'fi' is the presentation ligature ﬁ (U+FB01), one codepoint (sneaks in via PDF/OCR copy-paste). \w keeps it so 'paciﬁc' never matches 'pacific'; NFC/NFD leave it (only a COMPATIBILITY decomposition exists), so an NFC-only pipeline silently passes a non-matching token.
expectedHandling: Use NFKC (not just NFC) for the search key so ﬁ→'fi', ﬂ→'fl', ﬀ/ﬃ/ﬄ decompose → 'Pacific 231'.

trickyProperty: '(Taylor's Version)' designates a SEPARATE re-recording with its own MB release-group + an internal apostrophe. Strip-to-space splits Taylor's into 'taylor'/'s' and keeps version words; over-correcting bracket-strip collapses to bare '1989' grabbing the WRONG (2014) release.
expectedHandling: Treat it as a non-droppable discriminator: primary query keeps it (apostrophe removed without a space → 'Taylors Version', also try curly form); drop-the-clause '1989' is a LAST-resort fallback ranked below, never primary.

raw: All Too Well (10 Minute Version) (Taylor's Version) (From the Vault)
trickyProperty: Three chained parentheticals; '(10 Minute Version)' is load-bearing (vs the 5-min cut), 'From the Vault' is promotional. A greedy bracket-stripper removes all three, collapsing distinct recordings into an ambiguous 'All Too Well'.
expectedHandling: Parse each parenthetical independently: keep distinguishing clauses (10 Minute Version, Taylor's Version), drop promotional (From the Vault); emit a ranked variant ladder — full → minus Vault → minus Taylor's → minus 10-Minute → bare title (deep fallback).

trickyProperty: The LEADING '(What's the Story)' is part of the canonical title, not edition noise; plus internal apostrophe and trailing '?'. A bracket-stripper deletes it → 'Morning Glory' (a different real title), grabbing the wrong entity. Also a URL '?' delimiter.
expectedHandling: GUARD bracket-stripping: only strip a parenthetical matching the known edition/version/feat lexicon, NEVER a leading parenthetical or one whose words aren't in that lexicon. Normalize apostrophe by deletion (What's→Whats), drop trailing '?', URL-encode; always keep a full-title fallback.

raw: The Dark Side of the Moon (Remastered 2011)
trickyProperty: '(Remastered 2011)' is reissue noise with a 4-digit year a detector may misread; also appears word-order-flipped as '(2011 Remaster)'.
expectedHandling: Strip the whole remaster clause (remaster/remastered + optional year, either order) → 'The Dark Side of the Moon' primary; keep a remaster-preserving variant; don't treat a year adjacent to a remaster keyword as a title token.

trickyProperty: '(Mono)' denotes a genuinely different master that coexists with the stereo release as its own catalog entry — droppable noise in some contexts, a true discriminator in others. Blindly stripping it can grab the wrong master.
expectedHandling: Keep Mono/Stereo in the primary query (try '(Mono)' and bare 'Mono' forms); only fall back to dropping it if no qualified match; do not coalesce Mono and Stereo into one query that could match either.

trickyProperty: Apple/Amazon append ' - Single' (also ' - EP'); the ' - ' is the detection anchor. Strip-to-space → 'Easy On Me Single' where 'Single' is a literal token absent from Qobuz/Tidal, breaking cross-catalog match, and the anchor is destroyed.
expectedHandling: Detect and strip the trailing ' - Single'/' - EP' marketing suffix BEFORE general sanitization (the ' - ' anchor must survive) → 'Easy On Me'; keep the suffix only to disambiguate a single from a same-named album.

trickyProperty: '(10th Anniversary Edition)' contains an ordinal ('10th') mixing digits+letters; strip-to-space leaks '10th' as an orphan numeric token and over-constrains against the standard release.
expectedHandling: Strip the anniversary/expanded clause including its ordinal ('Nth Anniversary [Edition]','Expanded Edition') → standard title primary; emit the edition-preserving form secondary; don't let '10th' leak as an orphan numeric.

trickyProperty: The simplest, highest-frequency case: a single-word '(Deluxe)' suffix; strip-to-space → 'Take Care Deluxe' where 'Deluxe' survives, missing the plain 'Take Care' entry. The golden path a sanitizer must get right by default.
expectedHandling: Strip the trailing edition keyword family (Deluxe/Deluxe Edition/Expanded/Special/Bonus, including multi-word 'Super Deluxe Edition') → 'Take Care' primary, edition-preserving secondary. Pin this so the simple win never regresses.

trickyProperty: '(Radio Edit)' is a mix/duration variant; catalogs inconsistently call the same thing '(Single Version)','(Album Version)', or omit it. Strip-to-space over-specifies against the plain album cut.
expectedHandling: Treat Radio Edit/Single Version/Album Version/Original Mix as a synonym family: primary drops the clause → 'Wake Me Up'; emit mix-preserving variants for each synonym; rank bare title first for the standard track.

trickyProperty: '(Live Aid)' is a performance qualifier; live tags carry open-ended venue/year free-text ('(Live at Earls Court, 1981)') that rarely matches a title verbatim. Stripping can grab the studio cut when a live release was wanted.
expectedHandling: Recognize the live-clause family ('Live','Live at <venue>','Live in <city>','Unplugged','Acoustic') and strip for the primary studio-seeking query → 'Bohemian Rhapsody', plus a live-preserving variant; tolerate venue/year free-text inside the clause as non-title tokens.

trickyProperty: '(feat. Bruno Mars)' is attribution most catalogs store in a separate artist field; 'feat.' carries an abbreviation period. Strip-to-space leaves a junk 'feat' token and inflates the query with the guest name.
expectedHandling: Detect the feat-marker family (feat./feat/ft./ft/featuring) and split: primary drops the whole '(feat. ...)' clause → 'Uptown Funk'; a secondary keeps the guest as a free token (for title-embedded catalogs); route the guest into a performer hint, never the title-similarity compare; strip the marker word itself.

raw: I'm the One (feat. Justin Bieber, Quavo, Chance the Rapper & Lil Wayne)
trickyProperty: One parenthetical lists four guests with commas + '&', and the base title has an apostrophe (I'm). Strip-to-space splits I'm→'I'/'m' and lets four guest names drown the four-word title.
expectedHandling: Strip the entire feat(...) group as a unit regardless of internal commas/ampersands; preserve the apostrophe inside the contraction; base 'I'm the One' + ASCII-folded 'Im the One'.
